perturbation.original_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose;
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence but the Company is allowed to use the confidential information for other purposes, as long as it does not harm IHiS
perturbation.explanation: Modifying clause 3.1(a) to allow the company to use confidential information for purposes other than the defined 'Purpose,' so long as it does not harm IHiS, directly contradicts the original clause which strictly limits the use of confidential information to only the 'Purpose'. This introduces a significant ambiguity, as it is unclear what constitutes 'harm' and how it will be assessed. This contradicts the contract as a whole, but more in clause 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence but the Company is allowed to use the confidential information for other purposes, as long as it does not harm IHiS; (b) not to make any commercial use thereof; (c) not to use the same for the ben efit of itself or of any third party other than pursuant to a further agreement with IHiS; (d) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including with out limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one of the exceptions in Clause 1.1(c) ; (e) not to copy, reproduce , reverse engineer or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies, reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of IHiS; (f) not to disclose the same whethe r to its employees or to third parties except in confidence to such of its Representatives who have been informed of the confi dential nature thereof and who need to know the same for the Purpose and that: (i) such Representatives are obliged by their contracts of employment or service not to disclose the same or to use the same otherwise than for the Purpose ; and (ii) the Company shall enforce such obligations at its expense and at the request of IHiS in so far as breach thereof relates to IHiS' Confidential Information; (g) to be responsible for the performance of sub -clauses (a) to (f) above on the part
perturbation.justification: The changed text in clause 3.1(a) allows the company to use confidential information for purposes other than the defined 'Purpose,' as long as it does not harm IHiS. This directly contradicts the original intent and wording of the contract and specifically conflicts with the clauses (b) through (g) in the contradicted text, which place strict limitations on the use, commercialization, disclosure, copying, and reproduction of confidential information. By allowing use for 'other purposes' even if it does not 'harm' IHiS, the change undermines and contradicts these specific restrictions, creating a clear in-text contradiction and ambiguity about permissible use.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Company shall within seven days of : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause . Notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, the Company shall continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause s 3 and 4 above .
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Company may decide to keep documents and materials indefinitley : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause . Notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, the Company shall continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause s 3 and 4 above .
perturbation.explanation: Changing Clause 5.1 to allow the Company to keep documents indefinitely directly contradicts the original requirement that all confidential materials must be returned or destroyed within seven days. This undermines the confidentiality provisions and introduces ambiguity about the company's obligations regarding confidential information after the agreement ends. This affects the entire section regarding Return of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 The Company may decide to keep documents and materials indefinitley : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause . Notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, the Company shall continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause s 3 and 4 above .
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 5.1 mandates the Company to return or destroy confidential information within seven days. The changed text in clause 5.1, which is also the contradicted text, states that the Company may decide to keep documents and materials indefinitely. This directly contradicts the original requirement for the return or destruction of confidential information, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation provided further reinforces the fact that the change undermines the confidentiality provisions and introduces ambiguity about the company's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 IHiS reserves all rights in the Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no license is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any paten t, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by IHiS.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 IHiS reserves all rights in the Confidential Information but may allow the Company to use the Confidential Information. In particular, a license is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any paten t, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by IHiS.
perturbation.explanation: Clause 8.1 explicitly states that no licenses are granted. Altering this to now grant a license directly contradicts the initial assertion. This creates confusion about the intellectual property rights and the Company's ability to use IHiS's confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 IHiS reserves all rights in the Confidential Information but may allow the Company to use the Confidential Information. In particular, a license is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any paten t, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by IHiS.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that no licenses are granted under any intellectual property right. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that a license 'is hereby granted directly or indirectly'. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency as the agreement now both denies and grants a license for the same intellectual property rights. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, so it's evident that this change introduces a direct contradiction within the same clause, fitting the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
